events.summary: The Schwab Trading Activity Index (STAX) decreased to 50.10 in April, marking the largest monthly decline in a year, as retail clients became more cautious and reduced their exposure to technology stocks. This shift included a significant rotation out of higher-beta individual holdings into lower-beta ETFs, particularly in the Consumer Staples and Utilities sectors. While major indexes saw a rebound and oil prices remained high, clients were net sellers in nine of 11 S&P 500 sectors, indicating a dip in sentiment across all age groups and active traders.
events.title: Schwab Trading Activity Index™: STAX Score Drops in April as Retail Clients Cut Tech Exposure

events.summary: Canadian pension plans experienced tempered returns in the first quarter of 2026, with a modest median increase of 0.4%, due to geopolitical tensions, volatile markets, and ongoing trade uncertainties. Despite global events like the Middle East conflict and U.S. government shutdown, major equity indices (excluding the U.S.) generated positive returns, led by Canadian stocks. The Northern Trust Canada Universe tracks the performance of Canadian institutional defined benefit plans, highlighting the resilience of economies and the importance of sound investment strategies amid market fluctuations.
events.title: Canadian pension plans gained 0.4% in Q1 as wars, tariffs shook markets

events.summary: The private equity market in Q1 2026 is characterized by a widening valuation gap, with buyers and sellers struggling to agree on prices due to reemerging uncertainties like geopolitical instability and AI disruption. This situation leads to a repricing of risk by buyers and lenders, which is now impacting credit markets.
